findings.issue: Base URL input for Ollama/custom providers has no onChange/onBlur handler and no onSaveBaseUrl prop — changes are silently discarded on focus loss. The input is read-only in practice.
findings.recommendation: Add an onSaveBaseUrl prop to ProviderCardExpandedProps, wire it through ProvidersTabGrid/ProviderCard, and call it via onBlur on the url input. In SettingsModalContent call onUpdateProvider(id, { baseUrl: value }).

findings.issue: toolResponseStyle (settings.ui.toolResponseStyle) is defined in the store and has a full UI in BehaviorTab, but it is never read anywhere in the chat layer. The setting has zero runtime effect — tool cards always start collapsed regardless of the 'detailed' selection.
findings.recommendation: In ToolCallCard, read useSettings().settings().ui.toolResponseStyle and initialise the expanded signal to true when it equals 'detailed'. This is the key wiring step the recent sprint added the type and UI for but did not complete.

findings.issue: pushSettingsToCore() is a near-no-op stub (only syncs permissionMode). Five generation settings (reasoningEffort, thinkingEnabled, delegationEnabled, agentMaxTurns, agentMaxTimeMinutes) and agentBackend are persisted to localStorage and written to the Tauri FS, but there is no Tauri IPC bridge call that pushes these values into the running Rust AgentStack. The Rust agent will use its own defaults until restarted.
findings.recommendation: Add a Tauri command (e.g. apply_agent_settings) in src-tauri/src/commands/ that accepts the generation/agentLimits/agentBackend slice and calls into ava-agent's AgentStack to apply them. Call it from saveSettings() after writeSettingsToFS().

findings.issue: The 'YOLO' preset button sets permissionMode to 'bypass'. The type definition allows 'bypass' but the Rust CommandClassifier and PermissionLevel enum (per CLAUDE.md Sprint 60-04) only recognise 'ask' / 'auto-approve'. 'bypass' is silently unmapped when pushSettingsToCore() calls setPermissionMode().
findings.recommendation: Either add a 'bypass' variant to the Rust PermissionLevel and CommandClassifier, or rename the UI preset to 'Auto-Approve' and set permissionMode to 'auto-approve' to match the Rust side. Update the PermissionMode type accordingly.

findings.issue: onSetDefaultModel is wired in SettingsModalContent but ModelsListSection calls props.onSetDefaultModel?(modelId) without reflecting the change back to the parent store — the provider-card-expanded passes the callback but does not call onSaveApiKey / onUpdateProvider for defaultModel changes through ModelsListSection.
findings.recommendation: Verify that ModelsListSection calls props.onSetDefaultModel with the selected modelId and that the parent chain (ProvidersTabGrid -> ProviderCard -> ProviderCardExpanded) passes it through; add a missing onSetDefaultModel prop to ModelsListSection if absent.

findings.issue: GeneralSection does not expose any UI for settings.ui.hideThinking (defined in UISettings / DEFAULT_UI). The uiToggles array omits it. The appearance tab has a 'thinkingDisplay' control but hideThinking is a separate flat boolean that feeds into a different code path and is silently stuck at its default (false).
findings.recommendation: Add hideThinking to the uiToggles array in GeneralSection, or remove the field if it has been superseded entirely by appearance.thinkingDisplay.

findings.issue: agentBackend ('core' | 'core-v2') has a mutator (updateAgentBackend) and a default ('core-v2') but there is no UI control anywhere in the settings tabs to change it. The 'core' variant is now dead code per MEMORY.md (core-v2 removed), making this entire setting orphaned.
findings.recommendation: Remove the AgentBackend type, agentBackend field from AppSettings, and the updateAgentBackend mutator, or if 'core' variant is genuinely selectable, add a UI control under the Developer tab.

findings.issue: reasoningEffort and thinkingEnabled exist in GenerationSettings and are read by the toolbar strip (toolbar-strip.tsx) and right panel, but there is no UI in LLMTab or anywhere in settings to configure them persistently. Users can only change them via the inline toolbar, which does not persist across sessions.
findings.recommendation: Add a ReasoningEffort segmented control to LLMTab under the Generation section, writing via updateGeneration({ reasoningEffort }). This also auto-derives thinkingEnabled so both are persisted together.

findings.issue: delegationEnabled is read from generation settings to control the delegation badge in the toolbar, but there is no settings UI for it. Users cannot persistently enable Team delegation from the LLM tab.
findings.recommendation: Add a ToggleRow for delegationEnabled in LLMTab under Generation (or in TeamTab), writing via updateGeneration({ delegationEnabled }).

findings.issue: The meta key in defaultKeybindings (defined in KeybindingsTab.tsx) uses 'meta' string, but handleSaveKeybinding in SettingsModal normalises 'meta' -> 'ctrl' on save. This means the displayed shortcut ('meta') and the stored/matched shortcut ('ctrl') diverge visually on non-macOS systems.
findings.recommendation: Apply the same normalisation on load/display, or use a platform-aware key display helper so the UI shows 'Ctrl' on Linux/Windows and '⌘' on macOS consistently.

findings.issue: voiceDeviceId is defined in BehaviorSettings with a JSDoc describing it as the preferred audio input device, but there is no UI to select it. The voice-button.tsx component reads it from settings but the user has no way to change it from the settings panel.
findings.recommendation: Add a voice device selector under a 'Voice' sub-section in BehaviorTab, populated via the Web Audio API's enumerateDevices() or a Tauri IPC call.

findings.issue: The segmented button controls (Send Key, Tool Response Style) have no aria-label or aria-pressed attributes, making them inaccessible to screen readers. Buttons in a segmented group should be labelled as a group via role='group' with aria-label and individual buttons should use aria-pressed.
findings.recommendation: Wrap each segmented button group in a div with role='group' aria-label='...' and add aria-pressed={isActive} to each button.

findings.issue: The Thinking Display segmented buttons iterate with Array.prototype.map inside JSX, which is a pattern that defeats SolidJS fine-grained reactivity. Although the array is static here, it sets a risky precedent and differs from the For-based pattern used elsewhere in settings.
findings.recommendation: Replace the .map() call with <For each={thinkingOptions}> to be consistent with the rest of the settings codebase.

findings.issue: The MCPOAuthDialog is instantiated with hardcoded empty strings for authUrl and clientId, and a hardcoded scopes array. These values would need to be dynamically populated from the MCP server config, but no such data flows through the MCPServer type.
findings.recommendation: Extend the MCPServer type with optional oauth?: { authUrl: string; clientId: string; scopes: string[] } and pass it to MCPOAuthDialog, or fetch the OAuth config from the server at authorise-click time.

findings.issue: The version string is hardcoded as 'AVA v0.1.0-alpha' in GeneralSection. This will drift from the actual crate version unless manually updated.
findings.recommendation: Inject the version via a Tauri command (e.g. get_app_version) or from the import of package.json at build time, and display it dynamically.

findings.issue: The up/down reorder buttons use raw text '^' and 'v' characters instead of icon components, which is inconsistent with the rest of the UI that uses lucide-solid icons throughout.
findings.recommendation: Replace with <ChevronUp> and <ChevronDown> lucide-solid icons for visual consistency and add aria-labels.

findings.issue: The settings modal is rendered as a full-screen fixed overlay (fixed inset-0) rather than a proper dialog element. It has no focus trap — tabbing past the last interactive element exits the modal. It also has no aria-modal attribute or role='dialog'.
findings.recommendation: Wrap the modal content in a native <dialog> element or add role='dialog' aria-modal='true' aria-label='Settings' to the outer div, and implement focus trapping (e.g. via a FocusTrap wrapper or inert on the background).
